title: Used Crawler Excavator Sourcing Guide
shortDesc: How to inspect original main hydraulic pumps, diesel engines, and verify operational hours before placing orders.
content:
Used Crawler Excavator Sourcing Guide

Sourcing a pre-owned crawler excavator for international B2B buyers requires rigorous physical assessment to guarantee long-term operational efficiency. This comprehensive sourcing guide outlines the core verification standards for used heavy excavators.

1. Verifying Actual Operational Hours

Operational hours shown on the cabin meter are easily modified. True machine wear must be verified by secondary indicators:

  • Foot Pedal Wear: Excessively smooth or newly replaced travel and auxiliary foot pedals indicate a machine with over 5,000+ hours.
  • Bucket Linkage Play: Significant loose movement or excessive lubrication grease in the bucket pins and bushings usually correlates with heavy digging service.
  • Seat and Interior Condition: Original fabric wear on the operator's seat and wear on hydraulic joy-sticks reveal the cabin's true exposure time.

2. 120-Point SGS Hydraulic System Inspection

The hydraulic main pump is the heart of a crawler excavator. Always perform the following tests under operating temperatures (above 60°C):

  • Cylinder Response Times: Measure boom raising, arm extending, and bucket curling times to ensure they meet original factory specs. Delay indicates internal bypass or worn pump pistons.
  • Oil Leakage Mapping: Thoroughly inspect the main control valve blocks, rotary joint seal ring, and individual cylinder rods for hydraulic oil film.
  • Rotary Swing Stability: Perform full-load swing tests to inspect the swing motor's brake responsiveness and check for unusual mechanical gear noise.

3. Diesel Engine Diagnostics

Authentic brand engines (such as CAT, Komatsu, Isuzu) should be certified and undergo a thorough blowby gas and smoke color check:

  • Cold Start & Blowby: Open the engine oil cap during cold start. Excessive white blowby gas indicates worn piston rings.
  • Exhaust Smoke Color: Continuous black smoke suggests fuel injector malfunction; blue smoke indicates engine oil burning.

By enforcing these strict verification protocols, overseas importers can securely purchase direct-sourced pre-owned equipment with the highest ROI and original factory performance.

title: Used Mini Excavator Buying Guide
shortDesc: Best compact models for residential landscaping, municipal garden works, and rubber track maintenance strategies.
content:
Used Mini Excavator Buying Guide

Compact and mini excavators (under 6 Tons) represent the fastest-growing sector in urban construction and agricultural development. Selecting a pre-owned mini excavator requires distinct criteria compared to heavy mining rigs.

1. Choosing the Right Tonnage for Your Application

Mini excavators typically range from 1.5 to 5.5 Tons. Here are the optimal selection benchmarks:

  • 1.5 - 2.5 Tons: Ideal for indoor remodeling, pipe laying, garden digging, and tight spaces with width restrictions under 1.2 meters.
  • 3.0 - 4.5 Tons: Suitable for agricultural land grading, utility trenching, and municipal road repairs.
  • 5.0 - 5.5 Tons (e.g. Kubota KX155, CAT 305.5E): Offer the best balance of compact portability and structural digging force for medium landscape contracts.

2. Key Mechanical Inspections

Pay close attention to specific mini excavator weak points during your sourcing checklist:

  • Rubber Tracks vs. Steel Tracks: Rubber tracks minimize damage on paved urban streets but wear out faster. Check the rubber track lugs for deep cracks or exposed steel cables.
  • Swing Boom Play: Most mini excavators feature a swing boom joint. Check this pivoting joint for excessive horizontal play, which severely degrades bucket positioning precision.
  • Dozer Blade Cylinder: The front dozer blade takes heavy impact. Check the cylinder rod for scoring and ensure the blade mounts are completely straight.

Importers should prioritize established compact brands like Kubota and Caterpillar to ensure abundant supply of replacement components and high resale values globally.

title: Wheeled Excavators Sourcing Guide
shortDesc: Sourcing strategies for high-mobility utility projects, road construction, and urban maintenance equipment.
content:
Wheeled Excavators Sourcing Guide

Pre-owned wheeled excavators are highly prized by municipal contractors for their high road speeds and mobility, completely eliminating the need for low-loader transport trailers between job sites.

1. Core Mechanical Transmissions Inspection

Unlike crawlers, wheeled excavators feature complex drivetrain, axles, and braking systems that demand meticulous evaluation:

  • Transmission Gear Shift: Test the power-shift transmission across all speed ranges. Gear shifting should be immediate, smooth, and without transmission slip or clunking noises.
  • Axle Play and Oil Leaks: Inspect both front steerable and rear rigid axles for gear oil leaks at the hubs and differential housings. Check steering knuckles for play.
  • Outrigger & Blade Operation: Check the hydraulic cylinders of stabilizers (outriggers) and the front blade. They must hold high pressure under load without drifting down.

2. Tire and Wheel Assessment

Tire replacement costs on heavy wheeled excavators can be substantial. Evaluate tire wear meticulously:

  • Tread Depth: Ensure at least 60% of original tread depth remains for safe off-road traction.
  • Dual Tire Spacers: Many wheeled excavators use dual tire configurations. Verify that stone-guard rings are present between tires to prevent tire sidewall puncture damage.

Wheeled excavators are premium machinery assets. Direct sourcing from trusted yards ensuring original hydraulic blocks and certified transmissions guarantees project efficiency and lowest overhead costs.

title: How Much Do Used Excavators Cost?
shortDesc: Compare pricing across Caterpillar, Komatsu, and Sany by operating hours, manufacture years, and tonnage tiers.
content:
How Much Do Used Excavators Cost?

Pricing for pre-owned hydraulic excavators varies widely based on brand power, manufactured year, wear hours, and overall mechanical condition. Understanding price ranges is critical for B2B procurement planning.

1. Average FOB Price Ranges by Tonnage (Inspected Condition)

Below is a market index representing verified pricing for fully inspected, certified ready-to-work excavators:

Tonnage TierRepresentative ModelsPrice Range (FOB Used)
Mini (1.5 - 5.5T)Kubota KX155, CAT 305.5E$12,000 - $22,000 USD
Medium (12 - 15T)Caterpillar 312D, Komatsu PC130$25,000 - $38,000 USD
Standard (20 - 24T)CAT 320D, Komatsu PC200-8, Sany SY215C$38,000 - $58,000 USD
Heavy / Mining (30T+)Doosan DX300LC, CAT 330D, CAT 336D$55,000 - $95,000 USD

2. Key Depreciation Drivers

When assessing a used excavator's price, keep these key factor weighting indexes in mind:

  • Engine and Main Pump Hours (40% Weight): Machines under 3000h command a 30% premium. Over 6000h, price drops sharply.
  • Manufacturing Year (30% Weight): Newer models (under 5 years old) retain high value due to emissions tier compliance (Tier 3/Tier 4).
  • Undercarriage Wear (20% Weight): Brand new steel track shoes and chains add $3,000 - $6,000 value to a 20T excavator.

Working with direct-sourced exporters who provide certified inspection reports allows B2B importers to capture the best wholesale price-to-quality balance.

title: Used Excavator Shipping & Container Guide
shortDesc: Understand Flat Rack lashing, container disassembling rules, customs clearance paperwork, and global shipping fees.
content:
Used Excavator Shipping & Container Guide

Shipping extremely heavy machinery across continents is a highly specialized logistics operation. Choosing the correct shipping configuration saves thousands in freight costs and avoids port damage.

1. Primary Shipping Methods Explained

Depending on excavator tonnage and dimensions, there are three main sea shipping methods:

  • Roll-On / Roll-Off (Ro-Ro): The safest, most seamless method. The excavator is driven directly onto the vessel's enclosed deck. Ideal for premium heavy rigs but carries higher freight rates.
  • Flat Rack (FR) Container: Excavators exceeding 12 Tons are lashed onto flat rack platforms. Excellent for high-speed container ship dispatch. Demands highly professional steel-cable lashing.
  • 40HQ High Cube Container (Disassembled): The most cost-effective method for 5T - 20T excavators. The cabin, arm, boom, and counterweight are disassembled, carefully loaded and braced inside a standard container. Requires experienced engineers to load and reassemble upon arrival.

2. Professional Cargo Lashing and Protection Standards

Ensure your heavy exporter adheres to these strict lashing procedures before dispatching out of Shanghai or other major ports:

  • All-Steel Cable Lashing: Minimum 16mm thick high-strength steel cables secured to heavy deck-anchors.
  • Hydraulic Cylinder Protection: Exposed piston rods should be completely wrapped in anti-rust, shock-absorbent materials to prevent saltwater corrosion and damage.

Always verify your supplier provides direct ocean transport insurance and full customs documentation (SGS certs, export declarations, custom bills) for secure clearance.

title: Caterpillar 320D vs Komatsu PC200-8
shortDesc: Detailed technical comparison of fuel efficiency, hydraulic flow, cabin comfort, and aftermarket parts availability.
content:
Caterpillar 320D vs Komatsu PC200-8

The 20-ton class represents the absolute backbone of global construction. The Caterpillar 320D and Komatsu PC200-8 are undisputed legends in this tier. Let's compare them across core metrics.

1. Engine & Power Comparison

Both models feature exceptionally robust engines engineered for heavy construction duty:

  • Caterpillar 320D: Powered by the Cat C6.4 ACERT engine delivering 110 kW (148 HP). Known for immense torque, superior digging force in hard rock, and outstanding durability, though fuel consumption is slightly higher under full load.
  • Komatsu PC200-8: Powered by the Komatsu SAA6D107E-1 engine (110 kW / 148 HP). Exceptionally smooth power delivery, quieter idle, and famous for balanced fuel economy under standard digging modes.

2. Hydraulics & Control Response

Hydraulic flow control determines the excavator's actual speed on site:

  • CAT 320D: Employs high-pressure pilot-operated hydraulics. Delivers rapid joystick response and extreme breakout forces. The choice for heavy quarry and tough demolition.
  • Komatsu PC200-8: Employs Komatsu's patented HydrauMind closed-center system. Incredible coordination, offering unmatched precision and fine grading control for urban landscaping and trenching.

3. Technical Specs Comparison Matrix

Metric / FeatureCaterpillar 320DKomatsu PC200-8
Engine ModelCat C6.4 ACERTKomatsu SAA6D107E-1
Digging Breakout Force140.1 kN (Very High)138 kN (High)
Fuel Economy Index★★★★☆★★★★★
Aftermarket Spare PartsAbundant WorldwideAbundant Worldwide

Importers seeking maximum heavy-digging breakout force should choose the Caterpillar 320D; those prioritizing precision grading, lower noise, and premium fuel efficiency should go with the Komatsu PC200-8.
